question: According to the text, what is the primary requirement for a table to be in First Normal Form (1NF)?
options: It must have no transitive dependencies.
Each table cell must contain a single atomic value.
It must have a composite primary key.
All non-prime attributes must depend on other non-prime attributes.
hint: 1NF focuses on the structure of individual cells and the uniqueness of rows.

question: Under what specific condition is a table automatically in Second Normal Form (2NF) once it has achieved 1NF?
options: When the table contains no foreign keys.
When the table has a single-column primary key.
When all columns are of the same data type.
When the table has more than three columns.
hint: Partial dependency can only occur if the primary key consists of more than one column.

question: Which of the following is identified as a potential drawback of database normalization?
options: Performance overhead due to joining multiple tables.
hint: Think about how splitting data into many tables affects the speed of retrieving that data.
